reviews.review_title: The absolute greatest music rhythm game
reviews.review_description: HitStream - Demo Version is A) the absolute greatest demo available on Meta Quest, and B) HitStream itself is the absolute greatest music rhythm game on Meta Quest. No other game gives you the quality of music that HitStream does. No other game gives you the quality of gameplay that HitStream does. No other game allows you the level of gameplay-customization that HitStream does. The people behind HitStream recognized a fundamental truth about releasing a demo: give users a demo that is so robust and that is so rich in content and gameplay, that not only do users try it, but that they keep it installed on their device and that they come back to it again and again simply because it is so good! Want an intense workout where targets come at you from three surrounding gates and you have to dodge and squat obstacles as you punch to the beat? You can do that. Want a concentrated boxing experience with no squatting, little dodging, and targets come from one single gate in front of you? You can do that too (even when seated, which aligns with the user's position great)! Want to eliminate the voice of the trainer as well as sound effects so that the only thing you hear when you play the game is the music? You bet you can do that! HitStream - Demo Version gives you TWENTY full-length songs to play to, with at least 5 of these being full-vocal, commercially-viable music (it should be noted that whether the music be full-vocal or just instrumental, the quality of music in HitStream is studio-grade, which is important to mention given how many other music rhythm games out there currently rely on AI-generated BGM-style music, which often feels soul-less and empty). The charting and timing of targets to hit as it relates to the music being played is excellent. The demo also offers leaderboards to compare your score with other users, however these leaderboards do not sync with the leaderboards of the full-game.

Playing demo on a Quest 3 512. An orange artifact shaped like two octagons connected appears at the top of my vision. This is during the menu and play. I have emailed the devs (contactATand7limitedDOTcom) with screenshots asking for recommendations but received no reply. Hopefully they see this and maybe offer support of some kind.

I wanted to purchase the full version but not if I have to play with this giant orange shape floating above me during play.

Absolutely love Hitstream. And I’m so impressed they actually upgraded the demo! 
The options are many, beautiful scenery, great music, punches are perfectly timed plus a great all around workout. I share the link often! 
Beach Vibes is my favorite. 
Just waiting for a sale to get the full version. 
But truly, the care and quality they put into the demo, and willingness to share that free, is worth the full price for full version. 
(Hitstream should be out of App-lab and in the store!)

developer_response.body: Hi Patches4359 - thanks for the great review - we really appreciate your support.

reviews.review_description: I love this app!

developer_response.body: Hi @srmayol – thanks for the great review! :)

reviews.review_description: I use weights, kettlebells and bands but can't get myself to do cardio even though I used to be a runner. This game fills the need. I have the Demo but plan to purchase the full app soon. Is there any way to slow down the game to use as a warmup? Maybe in the paid app?

developer_response.body: Hey bobblickenderfer, thanks for the review. The Alpha levels can be quite nice for a quick warm up.

reviews.review_description:
Awesome fitness App would buy the full Version of it.

Please note 📝 
Don’t do the same as fitxvr with the Abo so people need to pay constantly 

Do one price and a lot people will love to buy this app.

When the full Version gonna be available?

Regards

developer_response.body: Hi Daveed2021, great to hear you're enjoying the game. You'll be pleased to hear that we are not planning on a subscription model. We're working hard on getting the full version ready at the moment, and we're hoping to release it 'soon'!

reviews.review_description:
I’m a regular user of FitXR and Audio Trip. Before covid I’ve been doing quite a bit of boxing and kickboxing in the gym. I also subscribe to Les Mills+ (on demand). 

I was looking forward to adding a new app to my regime and tried the hitstream demo.

It looks quite nice. The screen is mostly a 2D background, with only the floating arena rendered on top of it in 3D. This in contrast to FitXR and Audio Trip which are fully 3D. As said, it still looks quite nice.

After a few test rounds I have to conclude that everything is a little confusing. There is a mayhem of objects flying towards you, which somewhat reminds me of Japanese bullet curtain (bullet hell) shooters. All the objects have different things you need to do on them (punch, uppercut, hook, touch, and various weird high uppercuts and swings). There is a training session that explains them, although it uses confusing terminology (punch vs strike, which is weird, since a punch is a type of strike to begin with).

While the training session is doable, having everything thrown at once towards you in the actual levels makes you just randomly flinging your arms around, instead of the targeted moves you do in FitXR. Maybe it’s a flaw of the demo, and the full app builds it up more gradually.

Despite being no stranger to these types of apps, and both FitXR and Audio Trip being quite intense at times as well, I felt completely overwhelmed here.

Hopefully the developers can improve things here and balance the game out a little. I have high hopes that this will become a 5 star game in the future and will adjust my rating if it indeed gets better.

developer_response.body:
Hi topcodet52, thanks for leaving a review. Based on your feedback, we've simplified the terms used to describe the movements and we've updated the training level.

Our aim with Histream is full-body movement, which is why we have punches as well as swipes, catches, walls etc. Some levels do offer quite an intense workout. You might like some of the Alpha levels to start with as they're a bit gentler.

Each journey is choreographed, so the movements are all purposely mapped out. Some players might like to turn the 360' rotation off when starting out as the objects all then come from one direction.

Thanks again :)

reviews.review_description:
Might be the best app I have downloaded from app lab. I agree with everyone that this should be pay once rather than subscription. That said, I would gladly pay for this product. This free version is a very generous demo with lots of content so when it is time to pay, I don't mind doing my share for the developers. Speaking of developers as you read these reviews you can see how responsive they are to their community. This feels like it has the potential to become something more than just your average game or app. It almost feels like a partnership between the users and the developers.

Keep up the good work can't wait to play the updated levels.

developer_response.body: Hey PonyoKingOfDogs, thanks so much for the kind words. We're loving working with our growing community to refine the game and make it something really special. If you have any particular features you'd like to suggest, we'd love to hear about them on our discord!

reviews.review_description:
I’m extremely impressed with your app. Not to rush a good thing out, but can you please tell me when will you release the final product? I will GLADLY PURCHASE THIS APP ON DAY ONE WITHOUT ANY HESITATION WHATSOEVER. And I really enjoy your music selection, but will you also include away so I can also add my own music?? 

  Thanks very much for creating this product
You can really tell it’s a labor of love 👍👍

developer_response.body: Hi Parabow, we wish we could give you a definite date, but all we can say for now is that we're working as fast as we can! So pleased to hear you're enjoying Hitstream - it really is a labour of love for us. As a small indie dev team, the support we've received from the community means so much, and it's great to hear that the passion and enthusiasm we've put into designing the game is appreciated by our players. We won't be able to add custom music at this stage, but we may look into that at some point in the future. Thanks for the great review!

reviews.review_description:
This is my new favorite workout app! Nice work dev, and I am so excited to experience the full version. 

I am glad to learn that the plan is for this to be a one time purchase vs. subscription model. I don’t mind paying for packs/additional content, but don’t want to pay for ongoing subscription.

Happy gaming everyone!

developer_response.body: Hey BookerNerdsky, thanks for the review! Can't wait to show you guys what we have in store for the full release!

reviews.review_description: Please hurry and put the game up for purchase! I can’t wait!  I don’t use side quest so desperate to purchase this through the Oculus Store.. will it be this year?

developer_response.body: Hi TawseTerrier, We are working hard on getting the full version ready for purchase. We hope it wont be long and hope it will be this year!

reviews.review_description:
I enjoy the sense of openness in the settings for this game. Most fitness games I own are set in black or neon arenas, so this felt refreshing. I also enjoy turning around and the addition of the squat rings. 

I would like to put in a request, if it's not being considered already, for boxing modes or the ability to deactivate the slashing and high punch actions. I feel like there's too much arm swing and occasionally I feel a twinge in the back of my shoulder which alarms me. This tells me I may be overextending, especially into the high punches. And the swings feel kind of like throwaway motions that don't do much. If I could focus only on jabs, uppercuts and hooks, this would be a 5-star for me and one of my most used apps. So yeah, don't need to take those movements away, just provide the opportunity to deactivate them for those who don't want them. As I said, it's a nice change of pace from the darkness of other fitness games. Just need the gameplay to be a bit more controlled.

developer_response.body: Hey YiboYiboYibo, Thank you for your comments. We too wanted to make something that was bright and outdoor, unlike some of the other VR titles. We dont claim to be just about boxing, we want people to move as much as possible, bending, stretching and waving arms around - as well as punching and blocking. Thank you for your support!

reviews.review_title: Can’t adjust squat depth: big turn off
reviews.review_description:
I love the demo and would buy the full version if you could calibrate squat depth.
The reason why I am still sticking with supernatural is because Supernatural allows to calibrate squat depth so you don’t have to go all the way down.
You can turn off the squat depths I see in hit stream.  But I wish there was an option to adjust the squat depth so I can still get my squats in.
That is a turn off. Sadly many fitness games are that way right now.
Otherwise great game.

developer_response.body: Hi zycaria, thanks for the review. Based on your feedback, we've added a half squat option. You'll find it in the Player Preferences. Thanks again for your support.

reviews.review_description: It's mad good! Such a great activity game. It makes you push, hit, swing, doge, squat harder to beat your old result. Definitely worth the money when final version will come out.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great feedback Locooto, love hearing how much people enjoy playing Hitstream.

reviews.review_description: This was honestly an amazing twist on rhythm games that rewards you for force. With the different mechanics like punching different ways as well as having to do squats and twists and turns along with powerful and fast punches makes for a nice workout when I cant go to the gym along with just being a fun and very polished experience, I look forward to seeing the full release as this I'd already one of my favorite games in vr.

developer_response.body: Hey SubjectNova, Thank you so much, we are really pleased that you are enjoying all the aspects of our game. We wanted to make something for people to move whilst at home, and make it enjoyable! Thanks for your support! We hope to bring the full release soon.

reviews.review_title: Better than x-booster and fit xr
reviews.review_description: It is kind of mix of the advantages of the other two games (x-booster and fit X-ray). This, so much fun and the view is far much good look.

developer_response.body: Hey Nu.0800 - thanks for the review!

reviews.review_title: I would pay for the full version now!
reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun. I'm lazy and don't usually like rhythm games but this was fun. I guess my only suggestions would be in we could control the speed (super easy mode) and that the full game isn't out yet. After playing the demo I immediately tried to find the full game. Congrats. I'm sure it will get better with time but it is already great now!

developer_response.body: Hi ThurmanJ, thanks very much for the review :) We're working on a variety of different intensity levels for the full version of the game, so there should be something for everyone! One of the easier ones at the moment is the beach scene in the Original Alpha levels - well worth checking out! Great to hear you're enjoying Hitstream so far.

reviews.review_description: I never tried FitXR. I just heard that it went roge so I didn't even bother to try it.  Then I heared that there are few apps could possibly replace the one. Non of them really made me feel like doing exercise excet the thrill of fight. As soon as I tried this one I refunded other apps right away and just waiting for this one to be fully release.

developer_response.body: Hey Kimmybadger, Wow, really thrilled you like our game. We hope to release our Full version soon as its ready. Thank you for your support!

reviews.review_title: Great Game, Good Work out - Music ??
reviews.review_description: This is one of the best workout games, I hope very much it will NOT be another subscription game.  but.. here comes the but,  needs to be able to access personal music library.  Like the other fitness games out there, they get boring if you have to listen to the same 20 songs over and over.

developer_response.body: Hey Chintinhound, Thank you so much for your review! It's not our plan to become a subscription model. We do however want to release future DLC packs to increase playability over time. For the full release, we hope you won't be bored by the 90+ songs being planned, which have been carefully choreographed :)

reviews.review_description:
Fantastic! I have uncontrolled Refractory RA &my mom died so the stress makes my RRA worse.  It's difficult to find a workout that doesn't make me miserable. This gets me moving & doesn't make my RRA flare.  I told my doctor about it and he's pleased to hear that I am finally up and about! Thank you so very much.  Also,  the exercise may help with the depression following my mom's passing.  I'm looking forward to the full version. 
One suggestion: if it's not too difficult,  hand tracking would be great!
Thanks so much, 
SW

developer_response.body: Hey ShirlyOxendineWayman, we are so grateful that you have found our game helped you in this difficult time. We are working on the full version, and hope that gives you more variety of play. Hand tracking at the moment isnt where we need it to be, but if it improves then we will certianly consider. Thanks again for your support!
